description:
You are the release manager on a small team.  
Before today’s deployment window opens you must create a dedicated configuration directory and drop in a production-specific “.env” file.  
Once that is done, record a very small status log so that the build pipeline can automatically verify your work.

REQUIREMENTS  
1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/deployment_configs  

2. Inside that directory create the file  
   /home/user/deployment_configs/prod.env  
   with EXACTLY the four lines shown below (Unix line-feeds, no trailing spaces):  
   ─────────────────────────────────────────  
   APP_ENV=production  
   APP_DEBUG=false  
   DB_HOST=db.prod.internal  
   DB_PORT=5432  
   ─────────────────────────────────────────  

3. Create a single-line log file  
   /home/user/deployment_setup.log  
   whose only line must read (including the leading timestamp in ISO-8601 format down to seconds):  
   YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ  |  deployment_configs prepared  
   • Use the current UTC time when you run the command.  
   • Keep exactly two spaces before and after the pipe symbol (⎵⎵|⎵⎵).  
   • The automated test will accept any valid timestamp as long as it follows that precise “YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ” pattern and is within the last 10 minutes at verification time.  

CONSTRAINTS  
• All three steps can be completed with just two or three shell commands (mkdir, echo/cat, etc.).  
• Do NOT modify any other files.  
• The log file must contain only that single line—no extra blank lines.
truth:
Directory to verify: /home/user/deployment_configs

File to verify: /home/user/deployment_configs/prod.env  
Expected byte-exact contents (including final newline):  
APP_ENV=production\n  
APP_DEBUG=false\n  
DB_HOST=db.prod.internal\n  
DB_PORT=5432\n

File to verify: /home/user/deployment_setup.log  
Must contain one line that matches the regular expression (POSIX Extended):  
^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z  \|  deployment_configs prepared\n$  
and whose timestamp is within 600 seconds (10 minutes) of the test invocation time.
